identity.short_name: Capstone: reciprocity decides representability, norms construct it

problem_spec.summary: Capstone CHAIN: (1) Use Legendre symbols and quadratic reciprocity to decide a local representability condition (e.g., whether $-1$ is a quadratic residue mod $p$). This produces an interface quantity $T$. (2) Conditional on $T=1$, construct a normalized integer solution of a norm equation such as $x^2+y^2=p$ via Gaussian integer descent / quadratic form methods.
givens: Goal norm equation is fixed by mode (default: $x^2+y^2=p$) with normalization $x\ge y\ge 0$.
ask: Compute $T=(\!-1/p)$ using quadratic reciprocity and supplementary laws. If $T=1$, output the unique normalized pair $(x,y)$ with $x^2+y^2=p$. (Instances are chosen so that $T=1$ holds; otherwise the output is undefined and the instance is outside the family domain.)

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime whose representability is tested and then represented

parameter(s).name: mode
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the norm form (default: Gaussian norm)

parameter(s).name: normalization_rule
parameter(s).purpose: removes unit/order symmetries to make the representation unique

parameter(s).name: CRT_optional_glue_switch
parameter(s).purpose: optional: first construct a prime with desired residue class conditions using CRT, then apply the chain

invariant(s).name: interface_T
invariant(s).statement: Stage (1) outputs $T=(\!-1/p)\in\{\pm 1\}$, which decides whether $p$ splits in $\mathbb{Z}[i]$ and hence whether the norm equation $x^2+y^2=p$ is solvable.

invariant(s).name: norm_solution_uses_T
invariant(s).statement: Stage (2) (Gaussian descent / quadratic forms) is invoked only when $T=1$; it constructs $(x,y)$ from the splitting information guaranteed by $T=1$.

essential_assumption(s): Instances must satisfy the representability condition (e.g., $p\equiv 1\pmod 4$, equivalently $(\!-1/p)=1$) so that a solution exists.
Uniqueness relies on a normalization rule fixing sign/unit symmetries (e.g., $x\ge y\ge 0$).

If CRT_optional_glue is used, the constructed prime must meet both primality and residue constraints; the CRT stage outputs a candidate progression, and primality is ensured by the instance design (olympiad-style).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The coupling is global: $T$ computed in stage (1) determines solvability and is required for stage (2). When $T=1$, the norm method yields representations unique up to units; normalization fixes a single pair. Thus the final output is uniquely determined by the chain, not by independent subtasks.

technique(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
technique(s).purpose: evaluate the symbol using congruence class data

technique(s).name: Gaussian_integer_descent_optional
inputs: a nontrivial square root of $-1$ modulo $p$
technique(s).purpose: convert local data into an exact representation $p=x^2+y^2$ by descent on norms

theorem(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
theorem(s).role: computes $T$ efficiently and provides the local obstruction when $T=-1$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local quadratic-residue data determines splitting of primes in quadratic integer rings; norms then turn splitting into explicit representations.

critical_trick(s): Compute the interface symbol $T=(\!-1/p)$ using reciprocity (essentially $p\bmod 4$).
When $T=1$, use a square root of $-1$ modulo $p$ and run Gaussian descent (or UFD factorization) to produce $x^2+y^2=p$, then normalize.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Optional symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.

import_hint(s): from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Validate that the produced object(s) satisfy all defining congruences/divisibility/Diophantine constraints for randomly sampled parameters within the declared domain.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Cross-check the main result via an alternative computation (e.g., brute force for small bounds, or an independent formula such as factorization vs. valuation, or CRT reconstruction vs. direct search).

task(s).name: end_to_end_capstone_check
task(s).reason: For sample primes $p\equiv 1\pmod 4$, compute $T$ by Euler criterion/reciprocity and brute force a representation $x^2+y^2=p$ to confirm consistency and normalization uniqueness.
